What's Rule #7? Or any other rule?
Rule #7 for life!

Denis Phillips I have followed you since I moved to Florida May 30,2004 from Indiana. It was a summer to remember. But you were the only meteorologist that was giving facts and calm details. You didn’t create panic or fear. It was at that moment I decided suspender man would be my only go to for the weather. 20 years later and you are still my go to meteorologist because I know you are straight forward you keep your calm. If I ever see you freak out with Rule # 7 I may be back in Indiana. 😆 the funny thing is that first night we were all hunkered down at my nieces and I just felt a relaxing calm during it all. I did get an alarm call and had to drive to my work during Charlie. That was scary but I remained calm got what was needed done. I had started calling you suspender man that first night too funny. Please do not ever retire we need you. You are by far the most professional and knowledgeable meteorologist around. The only time I get scared is when Jim Cantore comes to town. I yi yi. 😆 thanks for being you and thank you to your beautiful family for sharing you with us.
